req.title: Procedure record
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-011 requires that a Procedure resource can be stored against an open/referenced Encounter via POST /Procedure. Three orthogonal test dimensions are covered: (1) EP positive — a well-formed request with a valid encounter reference returns 201 and persists the resource; (2) NEG negative — omitting the encounter reference triggers a 422 Unprocessable Entity; (3) BVA boundary — supplying a Procedure whose subject/encounter reference ID is exactly 1 character (minimal valid reference length) exercises the lower boundary of identifier length and must still succeed if the encounter exists, verifying the system does not silently reject minimal identifiers.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable at BASE_URL
A valid Encounter resource exists with id ENCOUNTER_ID
Clinician authentication token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: Build a FHIR Procedure payload referencing the existing Encounter
POST the payload to /Procedure with valid auth headers
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: encounter.reference in response matches submitted reference
trace: R-HLT-011: encounter open or referenced precondition satisfied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter_returns_201(base_url, auth_headers, existing_encounter_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/p-001"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{existing_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "80146002", "display": "Appendectomy"}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Procedure"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("encounter", {}).get("reference") == f"Encounter/{existing_encounter_id}"

tests.name: test_create_procedure_without_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable at BASE_URL
Clinician authentication token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: Build a FHIR Procedure payload with no encounter field
POST the payload to /Procedure with valid auth headers
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error detail referencing missing encounter
trace: R-HLT-011: error pathway 422 when encounter missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_without_encounter_returns_422(base_url, auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/p-001"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "80146002", "display": "Appendectomy"}]
        }
        # 'encounter' field intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    # Accept either FHIR OperationOutcome or a plain error object
    has_outcome = body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome" or "error" in body or "issue" in body
    assert has_outcome, f"Expected error detail in body, got: {body}"

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_minimal_one_char_encounter_id
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable at BASE_URL
An Encounter resource exists whose id is exactly 1 character (e.g. '1') — created via <create_encounter_fixture>
Clinician authentication token is available via <auth_fixture>
actions: Build a FHIR Procedure payload referencing 'Encounter/1' (1-char id)
POST the payload to /Procedure with valid auth headers
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: HTTP 201 Created (encounter with 1-char id is valid)
Stored resource id is present in response
trace: R-HLT-011: lower boundary of encounter identifier length
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_procedure_with_minimal_one_char_encounter_id(base_url, auth_headers, one_char_encounter_id):
    # one_char_encounter_id fixture guarantees an Encounter with a 1-character id exists
    assert len(str(one_char_encounter_id)) == 1, "Fixture must provide a 1-char encounter id"
    ref = f"Encounter/{one_char_encounter_id}"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "in-progress",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/p-001"},
        "encounter": {"reference": ref},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "71388002", "display": "Procedure"}]
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Procedure",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for 1-char encounter id, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Procedure"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body.get("encounter", {}).get("reference") == ref
